title: Auto-Fetch Reports and Dashboards on First Metrics Page Load
description:
Ensures that the reports and dashboards lists are automatically fetched from Salesforce when a user first accesses the metrics screen, without requiring them to visit the separate /reports or /dashboards pages first. A loading indicator (three green dots) shows during the fetch.

The auto-refresh only triggers once — when the local data tables are empty. Subsequent page loads use the cached data. The standalone /dashboards and /reports routes were removed since the data is now loaded contextually from the metrics page.
acceptance_criteria: Reports and dashboards auto-fetch from Salesforce on first metrics page load when local data is empty
A loading indicator displays during the Salesforce fetch
Auto-fetch only triggers once (when tables are empty)
ux_changes: Loading indicator during first-time report/dashboard fetch. Removed standalone report/dashboard page routes.
middleware_changes: Auto-trigger Salesforce sync for reports and dashboards when the metrics page detects empty data tables.
implementation_notes: One-time auto-fetch prevents repeated Salesforce API calls. Routes for /reports and /dashboards removed — all report/dashboard interaction now happens through the metrics sidebar.
comment_summary: Developer implemented auto-fetch with loading indicator and removed standalone routes. Pushed to staging for review.
